title: Chlorpyrifos Doubles Parkinson's Risk. A UCLA Study Mapped the Mechanism.
description: A landmark 2025 UCLA study — combining epidemiological data from 1,600 people with animal models — has pinpointed the precise cellular mechanism by which chlorpyrifos, still legally sprayed on American food crops, triggers Parkinson's disease: it disables the brain's protein disposal system, letting toxic debris accumulate until dopaminergic neurons die. With Parkinson's now the world's fastest-growing neurological disorder and chlorpyrifos registered in nearly 100 countries, the findings reframe a decades-long regulatory failure as an active public health emergency.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for A Common Crop Pesticide More Than Doubles Parkinson's Risk. Scientists Just Found Out Exactly How It Destroys Your Brain.
